comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
**Bug:** The `python-litestar` platform is missing from several product onboarding arrays in `platformCategories.tsx`, which will prevent its onboarding documentation from loading for Logs, Metrics, Profiling, and Replay. Severity: HIGH
Suggested Fix Add `python-litestar` to the `withLoggingOnboarding`, `withMetricsOnboarding`, `profiling`, `performance`, and `replayBackendPlatforms` arrays within the `static/app/data/platformCategories.tsx` file. This will register the platform for the respective product onboarding flows, allowing the documentation to load correctly.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: static/app/data/platformCategories.tsx#L121 Potential issue: The `python-litestar` platform has been added with declared support for Logs, Metrics, Profiling, Performance Monitoring, and Session Replay. However, it is missing from several critical registration arrays in `platformCategories.tsx`. Specifically, it is absent from `withLoggingOnboarding`, `withMetricsOnboarding`, `profiling`, `performance`, and `replayBackendPlatforms`. The onboarding hook `useLoadGettingStarted.ts` contains explicit checks that will prevent the documentation for these products from loading if the platform is not in these arrays. This results in a broken user experience where users can select these products in the UI, but the corresponding onboarding documentation will fail to appear. ```
Did we get this right? :+1: / :-1: to inform future reviews.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body:
### Litestar missing from logging and metrics onboarding sets **Medium Severity** `python-litestar` is added to the general platform list in `platformCategories.tsx` and has `logsOnboarding` and `metricsOnboarding` configs in its `index.tsx`, but it's not added to the `withLoggingOnboarding` or `withMetricsOnboarding` sets. `useLoadGettingStarted` checks these sets when `productType` is `'logs'` or `'metrics'` — if the platform isn't present, it returns `null` docs. This means the Logs and Metrics onboarding sidebars won't display any content for Litestar projects.
Additional Locations (1) - [`static/app/gettingStartedDocs/python-litestar/index.tsx#L24-L26`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/blob/31cf77d199d6a3a5f1c97bcad869dd99890342e1/static/app/gettingStartedDocs/python-litestar/index.tsx#L24-L26)

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body:
### Litestar missing from replay and feedback platform lists **Medium Severity** `python-litestar` is not added to the `replayBackendPlatforms` list even though the `index.tsx` exports `replayOnboardingJsLoader` and `feedbackOnboardingJsLoader`. Every other Python web framework (e.g., `python-fastapi`, `python-flask`, `python-starlette`, `python-django`) is in that list. Since `replayBackendPlatforms` cascades into `replayPlatforms`, `feedbackWidgetPlatforms`, and `feedbackOnboardingPlatforms`, the replay and feedback onboarding sidebars will fail to load docs for Litestar projects, and those two exported configs become dead code.
Additional Locations (1) - [`static/app/gettingStartedDocs/python-litestar/index.tsx#L17-L22`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/blob/31cf77d199d6a3a5f1c97bcad869dd99890342e1/static/app/gettingStartedDocs/python-litestar/index.tsx#L17-L22)

comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
**Bug:** The `python-litestar` platform is missing from the Python `platforms` set in `monitorQuickStartGuide.tsx`, causing it to be miscategorized in the Crons monitor form. Severity: MEDIUM
Suggested Fix Add `'python-litestar'` to the `platforms` set for the Python guide in `monitorQuickStartGuide.tsx` to ensure it is correctly identified as a "suggested" platform for Crons onboarding.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: static/app/data/platformCategories.tsx#L695-L701 Potential issue: The platform `'python-litestar'` has been added to several platform categories but was omitted from the Python `platforms` set within the Crons quick start guide (`monitorQuickStartGuide.tsx`). The logic in `monitorForm.tsx` uses `platformsWithGuides.includes(project.platform)` to determine if a project is "suggested" or "other". Because `'python-litestar'` is not in this set, projects using it will be incorrectly categorized as "Other Projects". This prevents users from seeing the Python-specific integration instructions for Crons, degrading the onboarding experience for Litestar projects. ```

comments.author: saponifi3d
comments.body: lgtm as a way to reduce load on the DB -- it'd be cool if we could determine a way to remove or reduce this being called though. mind also adding a backlog ticket so we can track this work for after launch? (can add it to the aci backlog milestone)

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**get_from_cache raises ValueError because DetectorGroup has no cache_fields defined**

The code calls `DetectorGroup.objects.get_from_cache(group_id=group.id)` but `DetectorGroup` model doesn't define a custom manager with `cache_fields`. The base `BaseManager` has empty `cache_fields` by default (line 93 in base.py), so `get_from_cache` will raise `ValueError('We cannot cache this query. Just hit the database.')` at line 291-292 of base.py - NOT `DetectorGroup.DoesNotExist`. This will cause all calls to `ensure_association_with_detector()` to crash with an uncaught `ValueError`.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read DetectorGroup model at src/sentry/workflow_engine/models/detector_group.py - no custom manager or cache_fields defined. Read BaseManager.get_from_cache at src/sentry/db/models/manager/base.py lines 283-296 - method raises ValueError at lines 291-292 when cache_fields is empty.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Use the original `.filter().exists()` pattern or switch to `DetectorGroup.objects.filter(group_id=group.id).first()` with a None check instead of get_from_cache.

```suggestion
    if DetectorGroup.objects.filter(group_id=group.id).exists():
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `LMC-Y4G`

comments.author: kcons
comments.body: actually, at this point this method is probably a no op. every error group should be created with a Detector Group. maybe we can also call it dramatically less

comments.author: saponifi3d
comments.body: agree that we could probably reduce the number of times we're calling this method, should that be a separate ticket / investigation so we can merge this and reduce the DB load while we investigate a more holistic solution?

comments.author: kcons
comments.body:
Ah yes, sorry, I lost track of this because my pr tracking filtered it for not indicating it needs review, will fix.

To close the loop on usage, current data shows us typically getting value out of this code 0% of the time, and peaking at around 0.00049% of the time. Which is to say, at this point, new groups basically always have a DetectorGroup.
We see a handful per day (9 in datadog typically, but we can't go lower) and yesterday was the only spike this month with ~200 (out of >250m).
So, I guess this code is useful for turning "basically always" to "always", though I'm not sure if that's worth enough to be running this as often as we are, but caching sure helps either way (assuming a decent hit rate).

comments.author: ryan953

Should we put some permissions checks around this? only admins will be able to edit integrations... I think we should hide or disable this button for regular users.

If a regular users clicks this they they'll endup at a page where they can't take any action, and they'll be take away from the issue page that they're presumably trying to debug :(

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**AttributeError on None when Group is not found**

The function uses `.filter().first()` which returns `None` when no record exists, but the `except Group.DoesNotExist` handler is unreachable because `.filter().first()` never raises exceptions. When `grp` is `None`, accessing `grp.qualified_short_id` will raise `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'qualified_short_id'`. This matches production bug pattern Check 5 (Type Errors) where None is used where an object is expected.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Verified that Group.qualified_short_id is a property on the Group model (line 764 in models/group.py). Confirmed that QuerySet.filter().first() returns None (not raises DoesNotExist) when no matching record exists - this is standard Django behavior. The except clause is dead code.

</details>

<details><summary>Also found at 1 additional location</summary>

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**qualified_short_id can return None, causing None to be stored instead of string**

The `_fetch_issue_labels` method stores `grp.qualified_short_id` directly in the result map (line 257), but `qualified_short_id` is a property that returns `None` when `grp.short_id` is `None` (Group model line 764-766). The return type annotation claims `dict[int, str]` but `None` values can be stored. When a group exists with a null `short_id`, the issue label will be `None` instead of `UNKNOWN_ISSUE`, potentially causing downstream type errors or displaying 'None' in the UI.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read Group.qualified_short_id property at src/sentry/models/group.py:764-766 which returns None when short_id is None. Verified short_id field is nullable (BoundedBigIntegerField(null=True) at line 680). Traced that _fetch_issue_labels stores this value without a null check.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Use UNKNOWN_ISSUE fallback when qualified_short_id is None

```suggestion
            resultant_map[grp.id] = grp.qualified_short_id or UNKNOWN_ISSUE
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `5MZ-KD3`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/snuba/occurrences_rpc.py:257:d5e5aa9d -->

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Missing organization scoping in Group lookup - deviates from established security pattern**

The `_fetch_issue_labels` method fetches Group objects using `Group.objects.filter(pk__in=group_ids)` without organization or project scoping. While the group_ids originate from Snuba RPC responses which are org-scoped, this deviates from the established security pattern elsewhere in the codebase (e.g., `Group.objects.get_issues_mapping()` at line 508 of organization_events.py) which validates `project_id__in=project_ids` AND `project__organization=organization`. The `qualified_short_id` includes project slugs which could leak cross-organization information if there's ever a bug in the Snuba scoping layer.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Verified by reading: (1) _fetch_issue_labels implementation at occurrences_rpc.py:252-258 showing unscoped Group query, (2) get_issues_mapping at models/group.py:608-622 showing the proper pattern with org/project scoping, (3) organization_events.py:508 showing usage of the proper pattern, (4) resolver.py:146-148 confirming RPC is org-scoped. The group_ids come from Snuba responses (not user input), so this is defense-in-depth rather than direct IDOR, but it deviates from established patterns.

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-security` · `LUC-QEQ`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/snuba/occurrences_rpc.py:257:0d04be99 -->

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Fix attempt detected** (commit 79f214b)

The fix adds project_id scoping to the Group query but omits the organization scoping component of the established security pattern described in the issue (which should include both project_id__in and project__organization filtering), representing an incomplete implementation of the proper defense-in-depth security pattern.

The original issue appears unresolved. Please review and try again.

<sub>Evaluated by Warden</sub>

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### New method duplicates existing `get_issues_mapping` utility **Low Severity** `_fetch_issue_labels` duplicates the existing `Group.objects.get_issues_mapping()` method on `GroupManager` (in `src/sentry/models/group.py` line 608), which already creates a dictionary of group IDs to their `qualified_short_id` values using the same `filter(id__in=..., project_id__in=...).select_related("project")` query pattern. The existing method also filters by `project__organization`, providing tighter scoping. Reusing `get_issues_mapping` and handling the `UNKNOWN_ISSUE` fallback at the call site would avoid this duplication.

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Cross-Organization Pipeline Access (IDOR) - Pipeline organization not validated against URL organization**

The endpoint retrieves a pipeline from session state via `_get_api_pipeline()` but never validates that `pipeline.organization.id` matches the `organization.id` from the URL. An attacker with access to two organizations could start a pipeline for org A, then call the endpoint with org B's slug in the URL. The permission check would pass (user has required scope on org B), but actions would affect org A's pipeline. This could allow advancing a pipeline or extracting pipeline step data across organization boundaries.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Verified by tracing: (1) ControlSiloOrganizationEndpoint.convert_args() (lines 265-321 in api/bases/organization.py) passes `organization` from URL to handler; (2) _get_api_pipeline() retrieves pipeline from session via pipeline_cls.get_for_request() which unpacks state.org_id (pipeline/base.py lines 61-96); (3) Neither _get_api_pipeline() nor the handler methods (get/post) compare pipeline.organization.id to the URL organization.id; (4) OrganizationPermission.has_object_permission() only checks user scopes on URL org, not that accessed data belongs to that org.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Add organization validation in _get_api_pipeline() to ensure the pipeline's organization matches the organization from the URL.

```suggestion
def _get_api_pipeline(
    request: Request, organization: RpcOrganization, pipeline_name: str
) -> Response | Pipeline:
            # Validate the pipeline belongs to the organization from the URL
            if not pipeline.organization or pipeline.organization.id != organization.id:
                return Response({"detail": "No active pipeline session."}, status=404)
        result = _get_api_pipeline(request, organization, pipeline_name)
        result = _get_api_pipeline(request, organization, pipeline_name)
```

<details><summary>Also found at 2 additional locations</summary>

- `src/sentry/api/urls.py:21`
- `src/sentry/api/urls.py:2035`

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-security` · `G4U-XQM`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_pipeline.py:50:74f5427c -->

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### ADVANCE response data silently overwritten by step info **Low Severity** When `api_advance` returns an `ADVANCE` result, `step_result.serialize()` may include a `"data"` key (if the step returned `PipelineStepResult.advance(data={"warning": "something"})`). The subsequent `response_data.update(pipeline.get_current_step_info())` overwrites this `"data"` key with the next step's data, since `get_current_step_info()` also returns a dict containing a `"data"` key. Any step-result data attached to the advance is silently discarded. Currently no step passes data with `advance()`, but the `PipelineStepResult` API explicitly supports it.

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**IdentityProvider.DoesNotExist not handled in _get_api_pipeline**

When `IdentityPipeline.get_for_request()` is called on line 49, it internally calls `Pipeline.unpack_state()` which performs `IdentityProvider.objects.get(id=state.provider_model_id)` without exception handling. If the `IdentityProvider` was deleted after the pipeline session was created (e.g., integration removed), this raises `IdentityProvider.DoesNotExist` and causes a 500 error instead of a graceful 404 response. This matches the "Missing Record / Stale Reference" pattern from real production issues.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Traced code path: _get_api_pipeline (line 49) → pipeline_cls.get_for_request() → Pipeline.unpack_state() (base.py line 84) → cls.provider_model_cls.objects.get(id=state.provider_model_id). IdentityPipeline sets provider_model_cls = IdentityProvider (identity/pipeline.py line 33), and this .get() has no DoesNotExist handler in the call chain.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Wrap the get_for_request call in a try/except block to catch DoesNotExist from the provider model lookup and return a 404 response.

```suggestion
from django.core.exceptions import ObjectDoesNotExist
    try:
        pipeline = pipeline_cls.get_for_request(request._request)
    except ObjectDoesNotExist:
        return Response({"detail": "Pipeline session references deleted provider."}, status=404)

```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `4XQ-VBX`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_pipeline.py:49:696c1a37 -->

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Pipeline initialized before API-readiness check causes side effects **Medium Severity** In `_initialize_pipeline`, `initialize_integration_pipeline` is called on line 114 before the `is_api_ready()` check on line 120. The `initialize_integration_pipeline` function calls `pipeline.initialize()`, which writes session state to Redis and records a `sentry.integrations.installation_attempt` metric. If the provider doesn't support API mode, the endpoint returns a 400 but the side effects have already occurred — the session is mutated (overwriting any pre-existing pipeline session for that pipeline type) and the metric is incorrectly incremented. The API-readiness check needs to happen before `initialize()` is called.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body:
### Pipeline stuck after finish error, step index out of bounds **Medium Severity** When the last pipeline step succeeds but `api_finish_pipeline` returns an error (e.g., permission denied, `build_integration` failure), `api_advance` has already incremented `step_index` past the end of `api_steps` and persisted it to Redis. Subsequent GET or POST requests to the endpoint will call `get_current_step_info()` or `api_advance()`, which access `api_steps[step_index]` — causing an `IndexError` and a 500 response. The pipeline becomes permanently broken with no way to retry the finish step.
Additional Locations (1) - [`src/sentry/pipeline/base.py#L287-L301`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/blob/4b7b02249047ba519547321eb633d8bfa0eb4ed1/src/sentry/pipeline/base.py#L287-L301)

comments.body:
### Property `is_api_mode` is set but never checked **Low Severity** `set_api_mode()` is called in `_initialize_pipeline` but the `is_api_mode` property is never read anywhere in the codebase. The `_get_api_pipeline` function only checks `is_api_ready()` (whether the pipeline *supports* API mode), not whether the session was actually *initiated* via API. This means a pipeline started through the regular web flow could be driven through this API endpoint without distinction, and the stored `api_mode` flag serves no purpose.
Additional Locations (1) - [`src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_pipeline.py#L119-L123`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/blob/4b7b02249047ba519547321eb633d8bfa0eb4ed1/src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_pipeline.py#L119-L123)

Location: src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_pipeline.py#L113-L120 Potential issue: In `_initialize_pipeline`, the `initialize_integration_pipeline()` function is called, which creates and persists a pipeline session in Redis. comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Missing KeyError handling for repos_removed dict access**

The code at line 138 accesses `repo["id"]` directly in a list comprehension for `repos_removed`, but unlike the `repos_added` handling (lines 117-121) which wraps `get_repo_config` in a try/except for KeyError, the `repos_removed` path has no error handling. If GitHub sends a malformed webhook payload where a repo dict is missing the "id" key, this will raise a KeyError and fail the entire task. This matches the KeyError pattern from webhook data access (SENTRY-3VCC, SENTRY-3ZXH).

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read the new file sync_repos_on_install_change.py and compared repos_added handling (lines 117-121 have try/except KeyError) vs repos_removed handling (line 138 has direct dict access). Confirmed the GitHubRepo TypedDict uses total=False making all keys optional. Checked data-validation.md reference confirming KeyError on webhook data is a known pattern (25 issues, 155,020 events).

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `N6G-NJL`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/integrations/github/tasks/sync_repos_on_install_change.py:138:e9ed05c7 -->

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body:
### Missing KeyError handling in repos_removed path **Medium Severity** The `repos_added` path gracefully handles individual `KeyError` exceptions per repo (lines 120–125), but the `repos_removed` path uses a bare list comprehension `[str(repo["id"]) for repo in repos_removed]` with no error handling. If any single repo dict is missing an `"id"` key, the entire comprehension raises a `KeyError`, and **no** repos get disabled — even the valid ones. This is inconsistent with the defensive approach used for `repos_added`.

### `GitHubRepo` TypedDict incorrectly uses `total=False` **Low Severity** `GitHubRepo(TypedDict, total=False)` marks all fields (`id`, `full_name`, `private`) as optional, but none of them are truly optional — the GitHub API always provides them, and the code unconditionally accesses `repo["id"]` and `repo["full_name"]`. This is the same anti-pattern the PR discussion flagged for `RepositoryInputConfig` (which was fixed to use `NotRequired`), but it wasn't addressed here. Additionally, this type is a near-duplicate of `GitHubInstallationRepo` in `webhook.py`, which correctly defaults to `total=True`.

**AttributeError when data.rule is None**

Lines 36 and 38 unconditionally access `data.rule.data.get(...)`, but `data.rule` is typed as `SerializableRuleProxy | None = None`. When `data.rule` is None, this will raise `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'data'`. The code on line 37 correctly handles the None case (`if data.rule else None`), but the tags and notes access on lines 36 and 38 lack similar protection.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read IssueNotificationData model in src/sentry/notifications/platform/templates/issue.py which shows `rule: SerializableRuleProxy | None = None` at line 64, confirming the field is optional. The render method has no upstream guard ensuring rule is not None.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Guard the data.rule.data access with a None check, consistent with the existing check on line 37.

```suggestion
            tags=data.rule.data.get("tags", "").split(",") or None if data.rule else None,
            notes=data.rule.data.get("notes", None) or None if data.rule else None,
```

This list is passed to the downstream `get_tags` function, which is not designed to handle this data type. The function incorrectly processes the first string in the list (e.g., `"environment"`) as an iterable, creating a set of its individual characters (`{'e', 'n', 'v', 'i', 'r', 'o', 'm', 't'}`). The renderer now reads them from `data.rule.data`, but `create_rule_instance_from_action` only puts `{"actions": [...]}` into `rule.data` — never `"tags"` or `"notes"`. comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Group.DoesNotExist not handled in IssueSlackRenderer.render()**

The renderer calls `Group.objects.get_from_cache(id=data.group_id)` on line 27 without handling `Group.DoesNotExist`. The `group_id` comes from `IssueNotificationData` created by the workflow engine, and the group may be deleted or merged between notification creation and rendering. This matches the Check 2 pattern (Missing Record / Stale Reference) with 81 issues and 1.4M events in production.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Verified: (1) Read IssueNotificationData in templates/issue.py - group_id is set from event_data.group.id during workflow invocation. (2) Read get_from_cache in db/models/manager/base.py line 323 - it calls self.get() which raises DoesNotExist on cache miss. (3) Read notification_action/utils.py - group_id is set at invocation time, rendering happens later. (4) Confirmed no DoesNotExist handler exists in IssueSlackRenderer.render() or its callers in service.py.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Handle Group.DoesNotExist by returning early or raising a handled exception that the notification system can catch gracefully.

```suggestion
        try:
            group = Group.objects.get_from_cache(id=data.group_id)
        except Group.DoesNotExist:
            # Group was deleted/merged between notification creation and rendering
            raise ValueError(f"Group {data.group_id} no longer exists")

```

The check `if std_key not in tags` performs substring matching on the string of tags (e.g., `"env" in "environment,level"` evaluates to `True`) instead of the intended set membership test. This causes incorrect tags to be included in Slack notifications, potentially showing misleading information to users when multiple tags are configured or when one tag name is a substring of another. ```

comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
**Bug:** The code splits comma-separated `tags` without stripping whitespace, causing tags with leading spaces (e.g., from "tag1, tag2") to be silently omitted from Slack notifications. Severity: MEDIUM
Suggested Fix Modify the tag parsing logic to strip whitespace from each tag after splitting the string. For example, use a set comprehension: `{t.strip() for t in tags.split(",")}`.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: src/sentry/notifications/platform/slack/renderers/issue.py#L34 Potential issue: When parsing comma-separated tags, the code splits the string by commas but does not strip whitespace from the resulting individual tags. If a user enters tags with a space after a comma, such as `"tag1, tag2"`, the resulting set will contain `" tag2"` with a leading space. A downstream exact-match check for the tag name will then fail, causing the tag to be silently omitted from the Slack notification. This leads to a loss of contextual information in alerts and is a regression from the previous implementation which correctly handled whitespace. ```

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Tag splitting omits whitespace stripping, breaking tag matching **Medium Severity** The new tag-splitting logic `set(tags.split(","))` doesn't strip whitespace from individual tags, unlike the old code which used `{t.strip() for t in blob.tags.split(",") if t.strip()}`. Both the `SlackIssueAlertHandler` and `DiscordIssueAlertHandler` in the codebase also strip whitespace when splitting tags. When a user enters tags as `"environment, level"` (space after comma), this produces `{" level"}` instead of `{"level"}`, which fails to match standardized event tag keys in `get_tags()`, silently dropping tags from the Slack notification.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Tag parsing drops empty-string filter from set comprehension **Low Severity** The old tag parsing in the factory used `{t.strip() for t in blob.tags.split(",") if t.strip()}`, which filtered out empty/whitespace-only entries. The new parsing `set(tag.strip() for tag in tags.split(","))` omits the `if tag.strip()` guard. For inputs with trailing commas or double commas (e.g. `"environment,,level"` or `"environment,"`), the resulting set will include an empty string `""`, which is a minor behavioral regression from the prior code.

comments.author: sentry-warden

**Organization scoping removed from Group fetch - defense-in-depth weakened** (`src/sentry/seer/autofix/on_completion_hook.py:238`)

The code change removes organization scoping from the Group.objects.get() call. Previously in `_maybe_continue_pipeline`, the group was fetched with `Group.objects.get(id=group_id, project__organization=organization)`. ### Timestamp update skipped if pipeline continuation raises exception **Medium Severity** The `seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered` update at the end of `execute` is placed after `_maybe_continue_pipeline`, which can raise unhandled exceptions — specifically, `trigger_autofix_explorer` at line 400 makes network calls that aren't wrapped in try-except. comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Group lookup not scoped by organization - potential cross-org IDOR**

The code fetches a Group by ID from `state.metadata.get('group_id')` without validating that the group belongs to the provided organization. While the Seer API is trusted, this is inconsistent with the existing pattern in `trigger_push_changes()` (lines 503-505 of autofix_agent.py) which explicitly validates `group_id == group.id`. If Seer ever returns incorrect metadata (bug or manipulation), this could allow updating `seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered` on a group in another organization, and potentially trigger webhooks/pipelines for the wrong group.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read on_completion_hook.py lines 81-99: group fetched with `Group.objects.get(id=group_id)` where group_id comes from `state.metadata`. Read autofix_agent.py lines 503-505: shows existing pattern `if group_id != group.id: raise SeerPermissionError` that validates the group belongs to the expected context. The new code lacks this validation before calling `group.update()` on line 99.

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-security` · `XH3-28A`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/seer/autofix/on_completion_hook.py:99:c288be21 -->

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Unhandled Group.DoesNotExist in completion hook causes webhook failure**

The `Group.objects.get()` call at line 85 does not handle `Group.DoesNotExist`. When an autofix run completes but the referenced group has been deleted (merged, archived, or removed) between run start and completion, this raises an exception. While the RPC layer catches `ObjectDoesNotExist` and returns a 404, this is inconsistent with line 82-83 which gracefully returns when `group_id is None`. A deleted group should trigger the same graceful skip behavior, not an error response to the webhook caller.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read the full file src/sentry/seer/autofix/on_completion_hook.py and traced the call path through seer_rpc.py. Confirmed that: (1) line 82-83 shows the intended behavior for missing group is graceful return, (2) Group.objects.get() has no try/except, (3) the RPC layer at seer_rpc.py:254 catches ObjectDoesNotExist but converts to 404 error response. The group_id comes from stored run metadata and the group can be deleted between run creation and completion webhook.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Use filter().first() with a None check to gracefully handle deleted groups, matching the existing pattern for missing group_id.

```suggestion
        group = Group.objects.filter(id=group_id, project__organization_id=organization.id).first()
        if group is None:
            return
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `2TA-F4L`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/seer/autofix/on_completion_hook.py:85:6c7b6a99 -->

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Unhandled `Group.DoesNotExist` exception in `execute` **Medium Severity** `Group.objects.get()` can raise `Group.DoesNotExist` if the group was deleted between when the autofix job started and when the completion webhook fires. The previous code in `_maybe_continue_pipeline` and `_maybe_trigger_supergroups_embedding` had try/except blocks handling this gracefully with logging and early return. comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
**Bug:** The `seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered` timestamp is updated for any autofix step, not just `ROOT_CAUSE`, which can permanently block the automated pipeline if other steps are triggered manually. Severity: MEDIUM
Suggested Fix Conditionally update the `seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered` timestamp only when the completed step is the root cause analysis step. This can be done by wrapping the `group.update(...)` call in an `if current_step == AutofixStep.ROOT_CAUSE:` block.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: src/sentry/seer/autofix/on_completion_hook.py#L85-L86 Potential issue: The code updates the `seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered` timestamp unconditionally upon the completion of any autofix step. The trigger logic uses this timestamp to prevent the automated pipeline from running more than once on a given group. The stated intent of the change was to update this timestamp only after a root cause is found. If a user manually triggers a preliminary step like `TRIAGE` or `IMPACT_ASSESSMENT` via the API, the timestamp will be set, which permanently blocks the main automated pipeline from ever running on that group, contrary to the intended behavior. ```

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Timestamp updated unconditionally for all step completions **Medium Severity** The `seer_explorer_autofix_last_triggered` update runs unconditionally in `execute` for every step completion, including TRIAGE and IMPACT_ASSESSMENT steps that don't guarantee a root cause exists. The PR description states the intent is to "only update when we know there is at least a root cause," but no check on the current step or root cause artifact existence is performed. Since TRIAGE and IMPACT_ASSESSMENT are valid API step choices, these completions will set the timestamp even without a root cause — which can affect `issue.seer_last_run` search filtering and post-process automation skip logic that relies on this field.

comments.author: jaydgoss
comments.body: Text renders as a `span` (inline) by default. As a direct flex child it gets blockified, which activates the `text-box-trim: trim-both` CSS on the Text component. That trims the half-leading from `line-height: 1.4` (`density="comfortable"`), reducing the element's height. Wrapping in Container keeps the span inline so `text-box-trim` has no effect and the full line-height is preserved.

**Bug:** Adding a value to a multi-select `CONTAINS` filter incorrectly changes the operator to `is` because the value check at `!token.value.value` doesn't handle multi-value token structures. Severity: HIGH
Suggested Fix Update the conditional check to correctly identify if a value exists for both single-value and multi-value tokens. Instead of only checking for `token.value.value`, the logic should also check for the presence of `token.value.items` with a non-zero length to prevent the operator from being changed incorrectly.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: static/app/components/searchQueryBuilder/tokens/filter/valueCombobox.tsx#L868 Potential issue: When a user adds a value to a multi-select filter using the `CONTAINS` operator, the operator incorrectly switches to `is`. This happens because the code checks for an existing value using `!token.value.value`. This check is valid for single-value tokens, but for multi-value tokens (which use a `token.value.items` array), `token.value.value` is `undefined`. This causes the condition to pass incorrectly when adding a value to an already populated multi-select filter, silently changing the search logic from "contains any of" to "is exactly one of". ```

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Typo causes None values to be passed when string "None" was intended**

Line 442 checks `values is not None` instead of `value is not None`. The `values` variable is a list initialized on line 419 that is always non-None, so this condition is always True. When `stringify_none=True` and `value` is actually None, the code will append `None` to the GroupBy instead of the string `"None"`. This defeats the purpose of the `stringify_none` parameter and can cause downstream Type errors when code expects a string.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read lines 410-445 of discover.py. Confirmed `values` is the list (line 419: `values = []`), `value` is the field value (line 431: `value = result_row.get(field)`). The condition `values is not None` will always be True since `values` is the list being built. Checked GroupBy TypedDict in timeseries.py which accepts `str | float | None` - the bug causes None to be passed when the string "None" was expected.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Change `values` to `value` in the None check

```suggestion
                values.append(GroupBy(key=field, value=value if value is not None else "None"))
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `SFZ-N36`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/snuba/discover.py:442:6c81d88a -->

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Typo: `values` used instead of `value` in condition **High Severity** The `stringify_none` branch checks `values is not None` instead of `value is not None`. Since `values` is the accumulator list (initialized as `[]` on line 419), it is never `None`, so the condition is always `True`. This means when `value` is actually `None` and `stringify_none=True`, the literal `None` is passed through instead of the string `"None"`, completely breaking the `stringify_none` behavior. Most callers use the default `stringify_none=True`, so this affects the common code path.

comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
**Bug:** In `create_groupby_dict`, the condition `values is not None` should be `value is not None`. The current code always evaluates to `True`, incorrectly handling `None` values. Severity: MEDIUM
Suggested Fix On line 442 of `src/sentry/snuba/discover.py`, change the conditional from `value if values is not None else "None"` to `value if value is not None else "None"`. This will correctly check the current field's value and convert `None` to the string `"None"` as intended when `stringify_none` is true.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: src/sentry/snuba/discover.py#L442 Potential issue: In the `create_groupby_dict` function, when `stringify_none=True`, the code intends to convert `None` values to the string `"None"`. However, a typo on line 442 causes the condition to check `values is not None` instead of `value is not None`. Since `values` is a list initialized as `[]`, this condition is always true. As a result, when a grouped-by field has a `None` value, it is passed through as `null` in the API response's `groupBy` metadata, instead of the expected string `"None"`. This is an unintended change to the API contract for consumers like the frontend. ```

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Unhandled SeerApiError in trigger_push_changes can cause 500 errors**

The new `trigger_push_changes` function calls `client.push_changes(run_id, blocking=False)` which can raise `SeerApiError` if the Seer API request fails (as documented in the method docstring and line 565 of client.py). Since this function is called directly from the API endpoint at line 264 of group_ai_autofix.py without exception handling, a failed Seer request will propagate as an unhandled exception causing a 500 error. This matches Check 6 (Internal API Request Errors) pattern.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read client.py push_changes method (lines 525-587) which documents 'Raises: SeerApiError: If the Seer API request fails' and confirmed the exception is raised at line 565. Read group_ai_autofix.py (lines 261-265) where trigger_push_changes is called without exception handling. The SeerApiError class is defined in seer_api_models.py with status code attribute for error context.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Handle SeerApiError in the function and log the failure gracefully, or document that callers must handle the exception. Given this is called from an API endpoint, handling at this layer with proper logging would be more robust.

```suggestion
def trigger_push_changes(group: Group, run_id: int) -> bool:
    """Trigger push changes for an autofix run. Returns True on success, False on failure."""
    from sentry.seer.models import SeerApiError

    try:
        client.push_changes(run_id, blocking=False)
        return True
    except SeerApiError:
        logger.exception("autofix.push_changes.seer_api_error", extra={"group_id": group.id, "run_id": run_id})
        return False
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `BX6-U62`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/seer/autofix/autofix_agent.py:503:4fc74d4b -->

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Cross-group IDOR: run_id not validated to belong to target group**

The new `open_pr` step accepts a `run_id` from user input and passes it directly to `trigger_push_changes()` without validating that the run belongs to the group specified in the URL. The `SeerExplorerClient` is initialized with `category_value=str(group.id)` but `push_changes()` only sends `run_id` and `organization_id` to Seer - it does not validate or include the category. This allows a user with `event:write` permission on one project to trigger PR creation on autofix runs belonging to groups in other projects within the same organization.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Traced the code path: (1) `run_id` comes from `request.data` via `ExplorerAutofixRequestSerializer` which only validates it's an integer, (2) `trigger_push_changes(group, run_id)` creates a client with `category_key='autofix', category_value=str(group.id)`, (3) `client.push_changes(run_id)` sends `ExplorerUpdateRequest` with only `run_id` and `organization_id` to Seer - no category validation. Read: group_ai_autofix.py, autofix_agent.py (trigger_push_changes at line 501-503), client.py (push_changes at lines 525-588), client_utils.py (ExplorerUpdateRequest). The same pattern exists for `coding_agent_handoff` (line 252-259).

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-security` · `W9N-9UA`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/seer/endpoints/group_ai_autofix.py:265:57066efd -->

### Counter increment via useEffect causes brief double-render flash **Low Severity** The counter increment in `makeSlotOutlet` uses `useEffect`, which runs asynchronously after the browser paint. However, portal creation happens synchronously during render once the target element is registered via ref callbacks. This timing gap means `slot.feedback.counter` is still `0` when the portaled content first appears, causing both the default `FeedbackButton` and the custom portaled feedback content to be visible simultaneously for one or more frames. Using `useLayoutEffect` instead would synchronize the counter update before the browser paint, preventing this visual flash.

comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
*Resolved in [`4ab51e1`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/commit/4ab51e11c88ed2b4e25991f57acc8d7e4e906e37)* **Bug:** Due to operator precedence, the expression `currentSlot?.counter ?? 0 - 1` is parsed incorrectly, preventing the counter from ever being decremented. Severity: HIGH
Suggested Fix Wrap the nullish coalescing operation in parentheses to ensure the correct order of operations. Change `currentSlot?.counter ?? 0 - 1` to `(currentSlot?.counter ?? 0) - 1`.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: static/app/components/core/slot/slot.tsx#L62 Potential issue: In the `decrement counter` case, the expression `currentSlot?.counter ?? 0 - 1` is evaluated as `currentSlot?.counter ?? (0 - 1)` due to operator precedence. Since `currentSlot` is guaranteed to exist at this point, `currentSlot.counter` is never nullish, and the expression always resolves to `currentSlot.counter` without decrementing. This causes the counter to remain stuck at 1 after a component unmounts. As a result, fallback components like the `TopBar` feedback button, which only render when the counter is 0, will be permanently hidden for the remainder of the user's session after they navigate away from a page that uses the slot. ```

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Operator precedence prevents counter from decrementing **High Severity** Due to operator precedence, `currentSlot?.counter ?? 0 - 1` is evaluated as `currentSlot?.counter ?? (0 - 1)`, i.e. `currentSlot?.counter ?? -1`. Since `currentSlot` is guaranteed to exist (checked on the line above) and `counter` is always a number, the `??` fallback never triggers—so the expression just returns `currentSlot.counter` unchanged. The counter is never actually decremented. Compare with the increment case which correctly uses parentheses: `(currentSlot?.counter ?? 0) + 1`. This breaks `Fallback` rendering: once any slot consumer mounts, the counter goes up but never comes back down on unmount, so `Fallback` content will never reappear.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body:
### Unregister action destroys consumer counter state **Medium Severity** The `unregister` reducer action sets `state[action.name]` to `undefined`, wiping both the `element` and the `counter`. The counter tracks mounted `SlotComponent` consumers. If a `Root` unmounts while consumers are still mounted, the counter is lost. When a new `Root` later registers, the `register` action falls back to `counter: 0` (since `state[action.name]?.counter` is `undefined`), even though consumers are still active. This causes `SlotFallback` to incorrectly render alongside existing consumer content, because it sees `counter === 0` and a valid element. The `unregister` action needs to preserve the counter (setting only `element` to `null`) rather than destroying the entire slot entry.
Additional Locations (1) - [`static/app/components/core/slot/slot.tsx#L161-L179`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/blob/b706bc5e0a63157c41abb9498a34a3dc08102039/static/app/components/core/slot/slot.tsx#L161-L179)

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Slot object not exported for consumer access **High Severity** `TopBarSlots` is declared as a private `const` (not exported), but the test file and the PR's intended API require consumers to access it as `TopBarSlot` (singular). The variable is neither exported nor named to match the import in `topBar.spec.tsx`, which imports `TopBarSlot`. Without this export, no component outside `topBar.tsx` can render content into the slots, defeating the core purpose of this PR.

comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
**Bug:** The call to `createPortal` uses `state[name]?.element` directly, which TypeScript cannot safely narrow to `Element`, creating a type mismatch. Severity: MEDIUM
Suggested Fix To allow TypeScript to correctly narrow the type, store the element in a local variable before the guard check. Then, use that variable in both the conditional and the `createPortal` call. For example: `const element = state[name]?.element; if (!element) { return ...; } return createPortal(props.children, element);`
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: static/app/components/core/slot/slot.tsx#L186 Potential issue: Due to the `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` TypeScript setting, the type of `state[name]?.element` is `HTMLElement | null | undefined`. A guard check on line 182 does not narrow the type for a separate, subsequent access on line 186 within the `createPortal` call. This creates a type mismatch because `createPortal`'s second argument expects a non-nullable `Element`. While the runtime logic prevents a crash, this pattern violates the project's type safety rules and should cause a compilation error. ```

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Reducer factory called inside component on every render **Low Severity** `makeSlotReducer()` is called inside the `SlotProvider` component body, creating a new reducer function on every render. Since the reducer is a pure function with no closures over changing values, it can be hoisted into the `makeSlotProvider` closure (outside the component) so it's created once per `slot()` call instead of on every render of the provider.

comments.author: JonasBa
comments.body: @cursoragent hoist makeSlotReducer so we don't call it on each render

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: The PR doesn't need a title or body update since I'm just fixing a performance issue that was already identified in the review. The change is now committed and pushed. ## Summary I've successfully hoisted the `makeSlotReducer()` call outside the `SlotProvider` component to fix the performance issue. **What I changed:** - Moved the `makeSlotReducer()` call from inside the `SlotProvider` component (line 198) to the `makeSlotProvider` function closure (line 197) - Now the reducer function is created once per `slot()` call instead of being recreated on every render of the provider **Why this matters:** The reducer is a pure function with no closures over changing values, so there's no need to create a new instance on every render. By hoisting it to the closure level, we avoid unnecessary function allocations and improve performance. The change has been committed and pushed to the `jb/pageframe/slots` branch.

**Bug:** The code re-raises a raw `requests.exceptions.ConnectionError`, which bypasses DRF's exception handling and results in a generic 500 error instead of a more appropriate response. Severity: MEDIUM
Suggested Fix Instead of re-raising the raw `requests.exceptions.ConnectionError`, convert it to an appropriate DRF exception, similar to how `Timeout` is converted to `RequestTimeout()`. Consider using a 502 Bad Gateway or 503 Service Unavailable status by raising a corresponding DRF exception to ensure the client receives a meaningful error code.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: src/sentry/hybridcloud/apigateway/proxy.py#L206-L209 Potential issue: In `proxy_cell_request`, a `requests.exceptions.ConnectionError` is caught and then re-raised. Because this exception is raised from the `ApiGatewayMiddleware.process_view` method, it bypasses the DRF exception handler and propagates up to Django's default handler, resulting in a generic 500 Internal Server Error. This is inconsistent with the handling of `Timeout` exceptions, which are converted to a DRF `RequestTimeout()` to produce a proper HTTP 408 response. This behavior provides a poor user experience when a downstream service has connection issues. ```
Did we get this right? :+1: / :-1: to inform future reviews.

However, `record_error()` calls `self.limiter.use_quotas()` and `self.limiter.check_within_quotas()` (via `_should_trip`) — both unprotected Redis operations that can throw. If Redis is flaky, an exception from `record_error()` in the `Timeout` handler prevents `RequestTimeout()` from being raised, and in the `ConnectionError` handler it replaces the original `ConnectionError` with an unrelated Redis exception.

comments.author: wedamija
comments.body:
Let's write this step like 
```
    operations = [
        migrations.SeparateDatabaseAndState(
            database_operations=[
                migrations.AddConstraint(
                    model_name="repositoryprojectpathconfig",
                    constraint=models.UniqueConstraint(
                        fields=["project", "stack_root", "source_root"],
                        name="sentry_repositoryproject_project_id_stack_root_so_c371dfa7_uniq",
                    ),
                ),
                migrations.AlterUniqueTogether(
                    name="repositoryprojectpathconfig",
                    unique_together=set(),
                ),
            ],
            state_operations=[
                migrations.AlterUniqueTogether(
                    name="repositoryprojectpathconfig",
                    unique_together={("project", "stack_root", "source_root")},
                ),
            ],
        ),
    ]
```

This way we create the new unique constraint first. The previous method drops the old unique index first, which leaves the table without the constraint until the new one comes in.

Severity: HIGH
Suggested Fix Wrap the `get_or_create` call in a transaction using `@transaction.atomic`. Alternatively, replace `get_or_create` with a safer pattern like checking `exists()` before calling `create()`, as demonstrated in `tasks.py`. Adding a `unique_together` constraint to the `PreprodComparisonApproval` model for the relevant fields would be the most robust solution to prevent duplicates at the database level.

Location: src/sentry/preprod/api/endpoints/preprod_artifact_approve.py#L65-L71 Potential issue: A race condition exists in the artifact approval endpoint. The `get_or_create` call on the `PreprodComparisonApproval` model is not protected by a transaction or a unique database constraint. If two approval requests from the same user arrive concurrently, both can pass the `get` check and proceed to `create`, resulting in duplicate approval records. Subsequent calls by that user will then trigger a `MultipleObjectsReturned` exception, causing an unhandled 500 error. The codebase in `tasks.py` explicitly documents this anti-pattern and uses a safer `exists()` then `create()` check to avoid it. ```

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body:
### Race condition creates duplicate approvers in response **Medium Severity** The `exists()` + `create()` pattern for APPROVED records has no deduplication on read. Concurrent requests (e.g., double-click) from the same user can create multiple APPROVED records. Unlike NEEDS_APPROVAL duplicates (which are cleaned up by `filter().delete()`), APPROVED duplicates are never removed. The snapshot GET endpoint iterates all APPROVED records without deduplication, so the same user appears multiple times in the `approvers` list. The referenced rationale in `snapshots/tasks.py` about duplicates being "harmless (cleaned up by filter().delete())" applies only to NEEDS_APPROVAL records, not to this case.
Additional Locations (1) - [`src/sentry/preprod/api/endpoints/preprod_artifact_snapshot.py#L343-L391`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/blob/95f4eb4bc3b376fb7d2797241e812f5574b8edd1/src/sentry/preprod/api/endpoints/preprod_artifact_snapshot.py#L343-L391)

comments.body: ### Multi-cell fallback silently returns 404 for old URL **Medium Severity** When `JiraSentryIssueDetailsView` is the view class and `len(cells) > 1`, the code falls through to `get_response_from_control_silo()`. Since `JiraSentryIssueDetailsView` is decorated with `@cell_silo_view`, executing it on the control silo results in a 404 (per `ViewSiloLimit.handle_when_unavailable`). The test for this case (`test_get_response_multiple_regions`) uses a mock `response_handler` that always returns 200, masking this production behavior. Once the feature flag is enabled and a Jira integration spans multiple cells, the issue details panel in Jira will break for requests arriving on the old `/extensions/jira/issue/{key}/` URL.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Unmatched bracket aborts scanning, skipping later valid JSON **Medium Severity** When `findMatchingBracket` returns -1 (unmatched bracket), the scanner pushes all remaining text as a single text segment and `break`s out of the loop. This causes any valid JSON appearing later in the string to be silently missed. For example, `parse [ error {"status": 500}` would treat everything from `[` onward as plain text because `[` has no matching `]`, even though `{"status": 500}` is valid extractable JSON. The `JSON.parse` failure path correctly advances just one character and continues scanning — the `matchEnd === -1` path needs the same treatment instead of giving up on the rest of the input.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Multiline JSON loses newlines during Prism rendering **Low Severity** `JsonSegment` renders each Prism line inside a `Fragment` with no newline character between lines. `usePrismTokens` splits on `\n` and consumes the newline characters during tokenization (in `splitTokenContentByLine`). So when a JSON segment contains actual newlines (e.g. `{\n "key": "value"\n}`), the rendered output collapses them — displaying `{ "key": "value"}` instead. The `white-space: pre-wrap` on `InlineCode` doesn't help because the `\n` characters are already gone. Other callers of `usePrismTokens` typically render a line break element between lines.

comments.body:
**Detector.DoesNotExist exception not caught - None check is dead code**

`Detector.get_issue_stream_detector_for_project()` calls `get_default_detector_for_project()` which uses `.objects.get()` internally (detector.py line 122). This raises `Detector.DoesNotExist` when the detector doesn't exist rather than returning `None`. The `if issue_stream_detector is None` check on line 819 is dead code - the exception will propagate as a 500 error before reaching it. If a project doesn't have an issue stream detector, API users will see an unhandled 500 instead of the intended validation error.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read detector.py lines 117-136: `get_issue_stream_detector_for_project` calls `get_default_detector_for_project` which uses `cls.objects.get(project_id=project_id, type=detector_type)` with no try/except. Django's `.get()` raises `DoesNotExist` rather than returning `None`. The None check in the hunk is unreachable.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Catch Detector.DoesNotExist and raise the ValidationError, or use filter().first() pattern

```suggestion
    try:
        issue_stream_detector = Detector.get_issue_stream_detector_for_project(project.id)
    except Detector.DoesNotExist:

```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `CQ3-PBQ`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/api/endpoints/project_rules.py:821:09a0a9b1 -->

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**IntegrityError when duplicate detector IDs passed to workflow create**

The `create` method iterates over `detector_ids` directly without deduplication (line 397), while `DetectorWorkflow` has a `unique_together` constraint on `(detector, workflow)`. If a user passes duplicate detector IDs like `[1, 1, 2]`, `bulk_create` will attempt to insert multiple records with the same `(detector_id, workflow_id)` pair, causing an unhandled `IntegrityError`. The `update` method correctly uses `set(detector_ids)` for deduplication at line 305, but this pattern was not applied to `create`.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read DetectorWorkflow model at /home/runner/work/sentry/sentry/src/sentry/workflow_engine/models/detector_workflow.py line 21 confirming `unique_together = (("detector", "workflow"),)`. Compared create method (line 397 uses raw `detector_ids`) vs update method (line 305 uses `set(detector_ids)`). Confirmed perform_bulk_detector_workflow_operations uses bulk_create without IntegrityError handling.

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `NC4-T46`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/workflow_engine/endpoints/validators/base/workflow.py:397:bd466b28 -->

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**TypeError when detector_ids is not provided in workflow create request**

Line 389 calls `set(validated_value.get("detector_ids"))` but `detector_ids` is an optional field (required=False in the serializer at line 68). When `detector_ids` is not included in the request, `.get()` returns `None`, and `set(None)` raises `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable`. This will crash all workflow creation requests that don't include detector_ids.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Verified by reading the serializer definition at lines 68-72 which shows `detector_ids` has `required=False`. Confirmed `.get()` returns None for missing keys. Tested in Python REPL that `set(None)` raises TypeError. The update method at lines 269-274 correctly checks `if "detector_ids" in validated_data` before accessing it, but the create method does not.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Use .get() with a default empty list, or check for None before calling set()

```suggestion
            detector_ids = set(validated_value.get("detector_ids") or [])
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `U6Z-3KU`

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Wrong item type check breaks initial keyboard focus **High Severity** `item.type === 'action'` will never be true. React Stately's `Item` components produce collection nodes with `type: 'item'`, not `'action'`. Other usages in the codebase (e.g., in `compactSelect/listBox`) consistently compare against `'section'` or `'item'`. Because this condition never matches, `firstFocusableKey` is always `undefined`, so the `useLayoutEffect` never calls `setFocusedKey`, and no item receives initial keyboard focus when the command palette opens.

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**P4Exception errors are incorrectly mapped to 404 regardless of error type**

The catch block converts all P4Exception errors to `ApiError(code=404)`, but P4Exception can represent connection errors, permission denied, authentication failures, server errors, and other issues that are not 'file not found'. The docstring explicitly documents that connection/command errors should raise `ApiError(500)`, but the implementation returns 404 for everything. This will cause callers to misinterpret server failures as missing files, preventing proper retry logic and error reporting.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read the P4Exception handling patterns in the same file at lines 193-243 which distinguish between SSL errors, authentication errors, and other issues with different ApiError/ApiUnauthorized responses and status codes. The docstring at lines 630-632 documents 404 for 'file not found' and 500 for 'connection or command error' but the implementation at line 650 uses 404 for all cases.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Distinguish between file-not-found errors and other P4 errors by checking the error message content

```suggestion
                error_msg = str(e)
                # "no such file" and "not in client view" indicate file not found
                # Other errors (connection, auth, permission) should be 500
                if "no such file" in error_msg.lower() or "not in client view" in error_msg.lower():
                    raise ApiError(error_msg, code=404)
                else:
                    raise ApiError(error_msg, code=500)
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `5G3-79L`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/integrations/perforce/client.py:650:f9e76747 -->

comments.author: cursor[bot]

The caller in `_fetch_file_from_scm` treats 404 as a silent `"file_not_found"` skip, while non-404 codes get logged as `"integration_error"`. This means real infrastructure problems are silently swallowed instead of being surfaced through monitoring. The docstring even documents that `ApiError(500)` is raised for connection/command errors, but no code path produces it.

Location: src/sentry/integrations/perforce/client.py#L655-L656 Potential issue: The `get_file` method in the Perforce client uses `p4.run("print", depot_path)` to fetch file content. However, the P4Python library, when in its default tagged mode, returns only a metadata dictionary in the result list, not the file content. The code incorrectly expects the result list to contain the file content at index 1. Because the list only ever has one element (the metadata), the check `if len(result) < 2` is always true, causing the function to incorrectly raise a 404 `ApiError` for any valid file. This prevents the source context feature from displaying inline code for Perforce projects. ```
Did we get this right? :+1: / :-1: to inform future reviews.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Empty file incorrectly raises 404 instead of returning empty string **Medium Severity** When a file exists in the depot but has no content (empty file), `p4 print` returns a list containing only the metadata dict with no content parts. The `len(result) < 2` check then raises `ApiError(404)`, telling the caller the file was not found. An empty file that exists in the depot is different from a missing file — the correct behavior is to return an empty string `""`. The source context caller treats 404 as `"file_not_found"`, so this leads to an incorrect error classification.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### CLI skips most default project settings on creation **Medium Severity** The command only calls `set_default_symbol_sources(project)` instead of `apply_default_project_settings(organization, project)` from `sentry.core.endpoints.team_projects`. The latter also applies default inbound filters for JavaScript projects, disables default detectors, configures Seer/ML similarity settings, autofix automation tuning, seer scanner automation, and auto-open PRs. Projects created via CLI will have materially different defaults than those created via the API.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Wrong return type annotation on `_resolve_organization` **Medium Severity** The `_resolve_organization` function is annotated as `-> None` but actually returns an `Organization` object. This causes the inferred type of `organization` on line 44 to be `None`, which means type checkers like mypy will flag every subsequent access (e.g., `organization.slug`) as an error on a `None` value. The correct return type is `Organization`.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Missing `user`/`user_id` causes silent signal handler failure **Medium Severity** The `project_created.send_robust` call doesn't pass `user` or `user_id`. The `record_new_project` onboarding handler defaults `user` to `None`, then hits `user.is_authenticated`, raising an `AttributeError`. Because `send_robust` catches it silently, onboarding tasks and analytics won't be recorded for CLI-created projects. All other callers of this signal pass either `user` (e.g., `AnonymousUser()`) or `user_id`.

comments.author: sentry[bot]
comments.body:
**Bug:** The `createproject` command calls `project_created.send_robust` without a `user`, causing a silent `AttributeError` in the `record_new_project` receiver when `user.is_authenticated` is accessed on `None`. Severity: HIGH
Suggested Fix In `src/sentry/runner/commands/createproject.py`, modify the `project_created.send_robust()` call to include a `user` or `user_id` parameter. For example, pass `user=AnonymousUser()` or the organization's default owner ID to align with other callers of this signal and ensure the receiver has the necessary context.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: src/sentry/runner/commands/createproject.py#L70-L74 Potential issue: The `createproject` CLI command calls `project_created.send_robust()` without providing a `user` or `user_id`. This triggers the `record_new_project` signal receiver with `user=None`. The receiver then attempts to access `user.is_authenticated` without a null check, raising an `AttributeError`. Because the signal is sent with `send_robust`, this exception is caught and silenced, preventing the CLI from crashing. However, this causes the remainder of the receiver's logic to be skipped, meaning project onboarding tasks and analytics events are not created for projects created via the CLI, leading to incomplete setup and broken user experience tracking. ```
Did we get this right? :+1: / :-1: to inform future reviews.

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Project.objects.create() can raise unhandled IntegrityError or MaxSnowflakeRetryError**

The `Project.objects.create()` call is not wrapped in try/except for `IntegrityError` or `MaxSnowflakeRetryError`. The Project model uses snowflake IDs (via `save_with_snowflake_id`) which can raise `MaxSnowflakeRetryError`, and there's a unique constraint on `(organization, slug)`. The existing API endpoint (`TeamProjectsEndpoint.post()` in `team_projects.py`) handles both exceptions by returning a 409 response with 'A project with this slug already exists.' In the CLI context, failure will produce an unhandled traceback instead of a user-friendly error message.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read `src/sentry/models/project.py` lines 369-418 confirming the unique constraint `unique_together = (("organization", "slug"), ...)` and the `save_with_snowflake_id` usage. Read `src/sentry/core/endpoints/team_projects.py` lines 236-246 confirming the existing API catches both `IntegrityError` and `MaxSnowflakeRetryError`. Read `src/sentry/utils/snowflake.py` confirming `MaxSnowflakeRetryError` can be raised.

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `4Y6-WZZ`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/runner/commands/createproject.py:66:e873d8df -->

This results in the symbol sources being written to the database twice for every project creation. The explicit call is redundant and can be removed.

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**TypeError when accessibleOnly=true is passed for non-GitHub integrations**

When `accessibleOnly=true` is passed as a query parameter, the code passes `accessible_only=True` as a keyword argument to `install.get_repositories()`. However, only `GitHubIntegration.get_repositories()` accepts this parameter. All other integrations (GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, Bitbucket Server, GitHub Enterprise, Perforce) have the signature `get_repositories(self, query: str | None = None, page_number_limit: int | None = None)` without `**kwargs`. This will cause a `TypeError: get_repositories() got an unexpected keyword argument 'accessible_only'` for any non-GitHub integration when the parameter is used.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read GitLab integration.py (line 163-165), Bitbucket integration.py (line 123-125), VSTS integration.py (line 310-312), GitHub Enterprise integration.py (line 218-220), Bitbucket Server integration.py (line 283-285), and Perforce integration.py (line 351-353). All have `get_repositories(self, query: str | None = None, page_number_limit: int | None = None)` without `accessible_only` or `**kwargs`. Only GitHub integration.py (line 312-317) has `accessible_only: bool = False`.

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `WHK-KRK`
<!-- warden:v1:src/sentry/integrations/api/endpoints/organization_integration_repos.py:78:bfea1a18 -->

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**accessible_only parameter is accepted but never used in GitHub Enterprise**

The `accessible_only` parameter is added to the method signature (line 222) but the implementation ignores it. Unlike the GitHub integration which checks `if not query or accessible_only:` to use the installation repos endpoint when `accessible_only=True`, the GitHub Enterprise version only checks `if not query:`. When a caller passes `accessible_only=True` with a search query, the method will still use `search_repositories()` which returns repos beyond the installation scope, defeating the purpose of the parameter.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read the full GitHub Enterprise `get_repositories` method (lines 218-245) and compared it with the GitHub integration's implementation (lines 312-353 in github/integration.py). Confirmed the endpoint at organization_integration_repos.py (line 70-74) passes `accessible_only` from query params. GitHub integration checks `if not query or accessible_only:` (line 328), while GitHub Enterprise only checks `if not query:` (line 224).

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Update the condition to use installation repos when accessible_only is True, and filter results locally by the query string (matching the GitHub integration behavior).

```suggestion
        if not query or accessible_only:
            repos = [
            if query:
                query_lower = query.lower()
                repos = [r for r in repos if query_lower in r["identifier"].lower()]
            return repos
```

comments.author: sentry-warden[bot]
comments.body:
**Calling .lower() on integer identifier crashes for GitLab integrations**

The code calls `r["identifier"].lower()` assuming `identifier` is always a string, but GitLab's `get_repositories()` returns `identifier` as an integer (`repo["id"]`). When a user passes `accessibleOnly=true` with a search query for a GitLab integration, this will crash with `AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'lower'`. The endpoint handles all `RepositoryIntegration` subclasses (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, VSTS), not just GitHub.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read GitLab's get_repositories() at src/sentry/integrations/gitlab/integration.py:163-172 which returns `{"identifier": repo["id"], ...}` where repo["id"] is the GitLab project ID (integer). Verified the endpoint uses `isinstance(install, RepositoryIntegration)` check which includes GitlabIntegration. Confirmed GitlabIntegration extends RepositoryIntegration at line 121-123.

</details>

**Suggested fix:** Convert identifier to string before calling .lower() to handle both string and integer identifiers safely.

```suggestion
                        r for r in repositories if search_lower in str(r["identifier"]).lower()
```

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `BMR-FV6`

**tags.split() crashes if tags is stored as list instead of string**

The `_extract_tags_from_rule` method returns whatever type is stored in `rule_actions[0].get('tags')` which could be a list, string, or other type. Line 50 then calls `tags.split(',')` assuming a string. If tags are stored as a list (e.g., `['environment', 'level']` instead of `'environment,level'`), this raises `AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'split'`.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

Read _extract_tags_from_rule at lines 56-66: returns `tags` from `rule_actions[0].get('tags', None)` without type validation. The return type annotation says `set[str] | None` but actual return is unvalidated. The example data in templates/issue.py line 82 shows tags stored as string 'environment,level', but rule data could contain any type. This matches the Type Errors pattern from the skill (Check 5).

</details>

Identified by Warden `sentry-backend-bugs` · `658-RSU`

### Discord test helper never sets tags on data object **Medium Severity** The `_create_data` helper accepts a `tags` parameter but only stores it inside `rule.data["actions"][0]["tags"]`. It never passes `tags` to the `IssueNotificationData` constructor, so `data.tags` is always `None`. The Discord renderer reads `data.tags` (not `rule.data`), so `DiscordIssuesMessageBuilder` always receives `tags=None`, producing no tag fields. *Resolved in [`ede5387`](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/commit/ede5387f328272b57ac786af4a8cd3abddffef7f)* **Bug:** The type check `isinstance(event, Event)` is incorrect because `get_event_by_id` can return a `GroupEvent`, causing missing context in Discord notifications. Severity: MEDIUM
Suggested Fix Update the type check to correctly handle both `Event` and `GroupEvent` types. Change `isinstance(event, Event)` to `isinstance(event, (Event, GroupEvent))` to ensure `group_event` is assigned correctly.
Prompt for AI Agent ``` Review the code at the location below. A potential bug has been identified by an AI agent. Verify if this is a real issue. If it is, propose a fix; if not, explain why it's not valid. Location: src/sentry/notifications/platform/discord/renderers/issue.py#L41-L43 Potential issue: In the Discord issue notification renderer, the `get_event_by_id` function can return a `GroupEvent` object. The code then checks `isinstance(event, Event)`. This check will fail when a `GroupEvent` is returned, as `GroupEvent` is not a subclass of `Event`. Consequently, the `group_event` variable will be `None`, leading to Discord notifications being sent without the intended event-specific context, degrading the quality of the alert. ```

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### isinstance check silently drops GroupEvent instances **Medium Severity** `Event` and `GroupEvent` are sibling classes (both extend `BaseEvent`), so `isinstance(event, Event)` is `False` when `get_event_by_id` returns a `GroupEvent`. This can happen for transaction-based events when `group_id` is passed. In that case, `group_event` stays `None`, silently discarding the event data and causing the Discord message builder to fall back to the group's latest event instead of the specific triggering event.

comments.author: cursor[bot]
comments.body: ### Empty notes string not normalized to None like tags **Low Severity** The `tags` processing correctly normalizes falsy values (empty string `""`) to `None` via `if tags else None`, but `notes` lacks equivalent normalization. When `action.data["notes"]` is `""`, `notes` is stored as `""` instead of `None`. The old Slack renderer explicitly handled this with `data.rule.data.get("notes", None) or None`, which converted empty strings to `None`. This normalization was lost during the refactor, creating an asymmetry between `tags` and `notes` in `IssueNotificationData` and diverging from the model's default of `notes: str | None = None`.
